hints: A list comprehension has the form [expression for item in iterable if condition]. Here: [word.lower() for word in words if len(word) > min_length].
The condition is len(word) > min_length — strictly greater than, not greater-than-or-equal. A word of exactly min_length characters gets excluded.
Wrap the whole comprehension in sorted(...) to get the alphabetical order: sorted([word.lower() for word in words if len(word) > min_length]).
If no words pass the filter, the comprehension produces an empty list []. sorted([]) is also [], so you don't need a special case.
